Tool Usage — Run #4 (max tools overall — no verifier pass) (60 calls)

Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Swift Relocations rate notice using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "pricing" or "surcharge"). Find Greg Pallone's April 11 email to Chloe Vance (subject: "Pricing Update Effective Immediately - DOT Documentation Surcharge") announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new bookings effective immediately and a $500 per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge, driven by retroactive placarding certificates, chain-of-custody logs, and hazmat declarations. The surcharge also applies to four in-progress shipments: Raj Patel, Emilia Cruz, Jordan Ekwueme, and Nora Bennett.
OE 2: Search emails for the Heartland Movers rate notice using search_emails (query: "Jake Loomis" or "Heartland" or "rate" or "Q3"). Find Jake Loomis's formal notice email to Marcus Thorne (CC: Chloe Vance) (subject: "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review") announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, covering linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, and accessorial charges. Jake ties the increase to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute and warns that continued uncertainty threatens MoveOps' preferred scheduling treatment for summer bookings.
OE 3: Search emails for Lakeline capacity correspondence using search_emails (query: "Sandra Walsh" or "Lakeline" or "backup capacity"). Find Sandra Walsh's reply to Chloe Vance (subject: "Re: Exploratory question on Q3 corporate relocation capacity") detailing Lakeline Logistics' coverage: Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX) at roughly 10% below current Heartland pricing. Cannot cover West Coast lanes, international shipments, or absorb a large share of volume away from one incumbent carrier at once.
OE 4: Search emails for Heartland Q1 dispute cancellation evidence using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "cancellation" or "Marchetti"). Find Chloe Vance's forwarded email to Marcus Thorne (CC: Hana Kim) (subject: "FWD: Cancellation evidence for Heartland Q1 dispute - Marchetti & Osei") containing the two cancellation emails sent to Jake Loomis: Lily Marchetti (RELOC-2026-009, cancelled Feb 10) and Danielle Osei (RELOC-2026-010, cancelled Feb 13).
OE 5: In the same email search results, find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute email to Jake Loomis (CC: Chloe Vance, Hana Kim) challenging invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 for $12,800. Marcus identifies 5 accepted moves ($8,000) and 3 disputed moves ($4,800): Marchetti (cancelled), Osei (cancelled), and Venkatesh (handled by Swift). Requests a corrected invoice for $8,000.
OE 6: Search Linear for the Heartland reconciliation ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or "reconciliation"). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (#342, "Vendor invoice reconciliation - Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)"), state: in_progress, assigned to Chloe Vance. Description details $4,800 overbilling for 3 items: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift. Payment hold placed by Marcus Thorne.
OE 7: Read Linear comments on the Heartland reconciliation ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon). Find Chloe Vance's comment (comment_chloe_heartland_recon_context) providing dispute lifecycle context: first 3 checklist items completed (Airtable verification, cancellation evidence, Swift/Venkatesh confirmation), remaining items dependent on Heartland's response. Notes that Q4 2025 (HM-2025-Q4-0031) was clean, 4 moves, $6,400, all verified, indicating a process gap on Heartland's end, not a pattern of overbilling.
OE 8: Search Linear for the broader vendor dispute workstream issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "billing discrepancies" or "consolidate"). Find linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118, "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response"), assigned to Marcus Thorne. Description reveals Hana expanded the review beyond the original 3 disputed items: 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves show discrepancies, Kevin Tran's file includes 2 days of storage billed without signed contract authorization, and Priya Nair's file includes a shuttle fee mismatch.
OE 9: Read Linear comments on the vendor dispute issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e). Find Hana Kim's April 14 comment: "this is broader than invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042. Three of the five Q1 Heartland moves have exceptions, and one of them is a storage charge with no contractual authorization." Also find Marcus Thorne's April 24 comment requesting three-bucket categorization: (1) items substantiable with contracts/shipment records, (2) items that look wrong but need ops confirmation, (3) items too weak to lean on in negotiation.
OE 10: Search Slack for Heartland and vendor-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "reconciliation" or "vendor rate"). Find Chloe Vance's message in #operations (C006) about doing the Q1 reconciliation on the Heartland bill and cross-referencing all 8 line items against the Relocations table in Airtable.
OE 11: In the same Slack search results, find Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005) about expanding the Heartland review beyond invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042: 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves have billing discrepancies, Kevin Tran's file includes an unauthorized $650 storage charge, and Priya Nair's file has a shuttle fee mismatch with origin access notes. Hana also flags that the earlier Linear dispute ticket has been untouched for two weeks and recommends consolidating into one position before going back to Heartland.
OE 12: Search Airtable using airtable_list_bases to find the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01). This is needed to retrieve relocation records for cross-referencing against vendor bills.
OE 13: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Relocations table (tblRelocations01). Find the Q1 cancellation records: rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti, Canopy Health, Nashville to Chicago, Status: Cancelled, RELOC-2026-009, Special Requirements notes cancellation sent to Jake Loomis on 2/10 with no vendor acknowledgment, Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers) and rec_reloc_osei (Danielle Osei, Vectral Systems, Phoenix to Denver, Status: Cancelled, RELOC-2026-010, Special Requirements notes cancellation sent to Heartland on 2/13 with no vendor acknowledgment, Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers). These Cancelled records confirm the dispute: both moves were cancelled in February but Heartland still billed for them on HM-2026-Q1-0042. Note: the Relocations table also contains separate later records (recReloc00000002 for Marchetti, recReloc00000004 for Osei) with Status: Completed and April 2026 move dates, these represent subsequent relocations for the same individuals, not the Q1 disputed moves.
OE 14: Find the Q1 Priya Venkatesh Airtable record (rec_reloc_venkatesh) confirming Status: Completed, Company: GreenStack Energy, Origin: Houston, Destination: Seattle, RELOC-2026-015. The Special Requirements field explicitly states "Moving Vendor: Swift Relocations" and "Handled by Swift Relocations (NOT Heartland)", directly confirming the third disputed item on the Heartland Q1 bill. The QuickBooks Swift bill (bill_swift_venkatesh, $4,450) provides additional billing confirmation. Note: a separate later record (recReloc00000005) also exists for Venkatesh with April 2026 move dates and detailed lab equipment handling notes but without the vendor assignment context.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching VendorRef "Heartland"). Find bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042) with 8 line items at $1,600 each ($12,800 total).
OE 16: In the same QuickBooks results, find bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), the annotated version with payment hold, showing 5 items marked [VERIFIED] and 3 items marked DISPUTED (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh).
OE 17: Find the Q4 2025 Heartland bill (bill_heartland_q4_2025, DocNumber: HM-2025-Q4-0031) showing 4 moves at $1,600 each ($6,400), fully paid (Balance: $0). This establishes the standard per-move rate of $1,600 and provides a clean baseline for comparison.
OE 18: Search QuickBooks for Swift Relocations bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching VendorRef "Swift"). Find bill_swift_tran ($2,885, Kevin Tran, Portland to Denver), bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450, Priya Venkatesh, Houston to Seattle, includes $650 electronics/lab equipment surcharge), and bill_swift_kovac ($3,675, Ines Kovac, Brooklyn to Austin, includes $350 walkup surcharge and $125 electronics handling). The Venkatesh bill confirms Swift handled this move, supporting the Heartland Q1 dispute.
OE 19: Search CRM for vendor company records using crm_search_companies (name: "Heartland") and crm_search_companies (name: "Swift"). Find Heartland Movers (description notes Q1 dispute and 20% Q3 rate increase) and Swift Relocations (description: "Recently increased rates and added a DOT documentation surcharge tied to post-audit paperwork requirements").
OE 20: Search CRM for vendor contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Jake Loomis") and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Greg Pallone"). Confirm Jake Loomis as Director, Corporate Accounts at Heartland Movers (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) and Greg Pallone as Regional Account Director at Swift Relocations (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com).
OE 21: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com. This is needed to send the consolidated ops data email.
OE 22: Search Slack for Alejandro's financial exposure work using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Alejandro" or "exposure" or "margin" or "vendor cost model"). Find Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance (C005) about starting the Q3 vendor-cost exposure model and flagging a data reconciliation problem: he can estimate directional risk from the vendor notices (Heartland +20% starting Q3, Swift +15% immediate plus $500 DOT surcharge per shipment), but cannot produce a defensible client-by-client margin forecast because the inputs live in three systems that do not reconcile cleanly (Airtable relocation records, QuickBooks bills, and CRM pipeline data).
OE 23: Search Linear for Alejandro's vendor margin dataset work using linear_list_issues (query: "Alejandro" or "vendor margin" or "consolidated" or "Q3"). Find linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (#214, "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM"), assigned to Alejandro Fuentes. Description notes Alejandro started the Q3 cost exposure model after the Heartland and Swift pricing changes and found there is no single source of truth for margin forecasting.
OE 24: Read Linear comments on the vendor margin dataset issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170). Find Alejandro Fuentes's comment (linear_comment_64ab4290e225) noting useful anchors already identified (Airtable relocation records, Hana's Heartland discrepancy work, prior Vectral account analysis) but warning that skipping the reconciliation layer risks presenting a false level of precision on client margin impact and negotiation options.
OE 25: Add a comment to the Heartland reconciliation Linear ticket (issue_heartland_q1_recon) using linear_create_comment, summarizing Hana's latest findings (dispute broader than original 3 items, 3 of 5 Q1 moves have exceptions including unauthorized storage charge), Marcus's three-bucket categorization framework, and the current dispute status including Jake Loomis tying Q3 rate increases to the unresolved Q1 matter.
OE 26: Post a vendor exposure summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006") covering both vendor rate increases (Swift's immediate 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge, Heartland's 20% effective July 1 tied to Q1 dispute), Lakeline's backup capacity limited to Midwest/Southeast corridors with no West Coast or international coverage, and practical implications for coordinators on upcoming move scheduling and vendor selection.
OE 27: Send an email to Marcus Thorne using send_email (sender: "chloe.vance@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"]) with consolidated operational data: Heartland Q1 dispute status ($4,800 overbilling for Marchetti/Osei/Venkatesh, payment hold in place, Hana's findings that the problem extends beyond the original dispute), Swift rate increase (15% base plus $500 DOT surcharge, effective immediately), Heartland rate increase (20% across all tiers, effective July 1, tied to dispute), Lakeline backup capacity (Midwest/Southeast corridors only, ~10% below Heartland pricing, cannot cover West Coast or international), and Alejandro's financial exposure status (Q3 vendor-cost exposure model started but flagging that a defensible client-by-client margin forecast is not yet possible because Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM data do not reconcile cleanly, output is currently directional ranges and assumptions, not final numbers).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Swift Relocations rate notice using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "pricing" or "surcharge"). Find Greg Pallone's April 11 email to Chloe Vance (subject: "Pricing Update Effective Immediately - DOT Documentation Surcharge") announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new bookings effective immediately and a $500 per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge, driven by retroactive placarding certificates, chain-of-custody logs, and hazmat declarations. The surcharge also applies to four in-progress shipments: Raj Patel, Emilia Cruz, Jordan Ekwueme, and Nora Bennett.
OE 2: Search emails for the Heartland Movers rate notice using search_emails (query: "Jake Loomis" or "Heartland" or "rate" or "Q3"). Find Jake Loomis's formal notice email to Marcus Thorne (CC: Chloe Vance) (subject: "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review") announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, covering linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, and accessorial charges. Jake ties the increase to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute and warns that continued uncertainty threatens MoveOps' preferred scheduling treatment for summer bookings.
OE 3: Search emails for Lakeline capacity correspondence using search_emails (query: "Sandra Walsh" or "Lakeline" or "backup capacity"). Find Sandra Walsh's reply to Chloe Vance (subject: "Re: Exploratory question on Q3 corporate relocation capacity") detailing Lakeline Logistics' coverage: Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX) at roughly 10% below current Heartland pricing. Cannot cover West Coast lanes, international shipments, or absorb a large share of volume away from one incumbent carrier at once.
OE 4: Search emails for Heartland Q1 dispute cancellation evidence using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "cancellation" or "Marchetti"). Find Chloe Vance's forwarded email to Marcus Thorne (CC: Hana Kim) (subject: "FWD: Cancellation evidence for Heartland Q1 dispute - Marchetti & Osei") containing the two cancellation emails sent to Jake Loomis: Lily Marchetti (RELOC-2026-009, cancelled Feb 10) and Danielle Osei (RELOC-2026-010, cancelled Feb 13).
OE 5: In the same email search results, find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute email to Jake Loomis (CC: Chloe Vance, Hana Kim) challenging invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 for $12,800. Marcus identifies 5 accepted moves ($8,000) and 3 disputed moves ($4,800): Marchetti (cancelled), Osei (cancelled), and Venkatesh (handled by Swift). Requests a corrected invoice for $8,000.
OE 6: Search Linear for the Heartland reconciliation ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or "reconciliation"). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (#342, "Vendor invoice reconciliation - Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)"), state: in_progress, assigned to Chloe Vance. Description details $4,800 overbilling for 3 items: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift. Payment hold placed by Marcus Thorne.
OE 7: Read Linear comments on the Heartland reconciliation ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon). Find Chloe Vance's comment (comment_chloe_heartland_recon_context) providing dispute lifecycle context: first 3 checklist items completed (Airtable verification, cancellation evidence, Swift/Venkatesh confirmation), remaining items dependent on Heartland's response. Notes that Q4 2025 (HM-2025-Q4-0031) was clean, 4 moves, $6,400, all verified, indicating a process gap on Heartland's end, not a pattern of overbilling.
OE 8: Search Linear for the broader vendor dispute workstream issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "billing discrepancies" or "consolidate"). Find linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118, "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response"), assigned to Marcus Thorne. Description reveals Hana expanded the review beyond the original 3 disputed items: 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves show discrepancies, Kevin Tran's file includes 2 days of storage billed without signed contract authorization, and Priya Nair's file includes a shuttle fee mismatch.
OE 9: Read Linear comments on the vendor dispute issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e). Three of the five Q1 Heartland moves have exceptions, and one of them is a storage charge with no contractual authorization." Also find Marcus Thorne's April 24 comment requesting three-bucket categorization: (1) items substantiable with contracts/shipment records, (2) items that look wrong but need ops confirmation, (3) items too weak to lean on in negotiation.
OE 10: Search Slack for Heartland and vendor-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "reconciliation" or "vendor rate"). OE 11: In the same Slack search results, find Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005) about expanding the Heartland review beyond invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042: 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves have billing discrepancies, Kevin Tran's file includes an unauthorized $650 storage charge, and Priya Nair's file has a shuttle fee mismatch with origin access notes. Hana also flags that the earlier Linear dispute ticket has been untouched for two weeks and recommends consolidating into one position before going back to Heartland.
OE 12: Search Airtable using airtable_list_bases to find the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01). This is needed to retrieve relocation records for cross-referencing against vendor bills.
OE 13: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Relocations table (tblRelocations01). Find the Q1 cancellation records: rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti, Canopy Health, Nashville to Chicago, Status: Cancelled, RELOC-2026-009, Special Requirements notes cancellation sent to Jake Loomis on 2/10 with no vendor acknowledgment, Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers) and rec_reloc_osei (Danielle Osei, Vectral Systems, Phoenix to Denver, Status: Cancelled, RELOC-2026-010, Special Requirements notes cancellation sent to Heartland on 2/13 with no vendor acknowledgment, Moving Vendor: Heartland Movers). OE 14: Find the Q1 Priya Venkatesh Airtable record (rec_reloc_venkatesh) confirming Status: Completed, Company: GreenStack Energy, Origin: Houston, Destination: Seattle, RELOC-2026-015. The Special Requirements field explicitly states "Moving Vendor: Swift Relocations" and "Handled by Swift Relocations (NOT Heartland)", directly confirming the third disputed item on the Heartland Q1 bill. The QuickBooks Swift bill (bill_swift_venkatesh, $4,450) provides additional billing confirmation. OE 15: Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching VendorRef "Heartland"). Find bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042) with 8 line items at $1,600 each ($12,800 total).
OE 16: In the same QuickBooks results, find bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), the annotated version with payment hold, showing 5 items marked [VERIFIED] and 3 items marked DISPUTED (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh).
OE 17: Find the Q4 2025 Heartland bill (bill_heartland_q4_2025, DocNumber: HM-2025-Q4-0031) showing 4 moves at $1,600 each ($6,400), fully paid (Balance: $0). This establishes the standard per-move rate of $1,600 and provides a clean baseline for comparison.
OE 18: Search QuickBooks for Swift Relocations bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching VendorRef "Swift"). Find bill_swift_tran ($2,885, Kevin Tran, Portland to Denver), bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450, Priya Venkatesh, Houston to Seattle, includes $650 electronics/lab equipment surcharge), and bill_swift_kovac ($3,675, Ines Kovac, Brooklyn to Austin, includes $350 walkup surcharge and $125 electronics handling). The Venkatesh bill confirms Swift handled this move, supporting the Heartland Q1 dispute.
OE 19: Search CRM for vendor company records using crm_search_companies (name: "Heartland") and crm_search_companies (name: "Swift"). Find Heartland Movers (description notes Q1 dispute and 20% Q3 rate increase) and Swift Relocations (description: "Recently increased rates and added a DOT documentation surcharge tied to post-audit paperwork requirements").
OE 20: Search CRM for vendor contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Jake Loomis") and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Greg Pallone"). Confirm Jake Loomis as Director, Corporate Accounts at Heartland Movers (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) and Greg Pallone as Regional Account Director at Swift Relocations (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com).
OE 21: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com. This is needed to send the consolidated ops data email.
OE 22: Search Slack for Alejandro's financial exposure work using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Alejandro" or "exposure" or "margin" or "vendor cost model"). Find Alejandro Fuentes's message in #finance (C005) about starting the Q3 vendor-cost exposure model and flagging a data reconciliation problem: he can estimate directional risk from the vendor notices (Heartland +20% starting Q3, Swift +15% immediate plus $500 DOT surcharge per shipment), but cannot produce a defensible client-by-client margin forecast because the inputs live in three systems that do not reconcile cleanly (Airtable relocation records, QuickBooks bills, and CRM pipeline data).
OE 23: Search Linear for Alejandro's vendor margin dataset work using linear_list_issues (query: "Alejandro" or "vendor margin" or "consolidated" or "Q3"). Find linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (#214, "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM"), assigned to Alejandro Fuentes. Description notes Alejandro started the Q3 cost exposure model after the Heartland and Swift pricing changes and found there is no single source of truth for margin forecasting.
OE 24: Read Linear comments on the vendor margin dataset issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170). Find Alejandro Fuentes's comment (linear_comment_64ab4290e225) noting useful anchors already identified (Airtable relocation records, Hana's Heartland discrepancy work, prior Vectral account analysis) but warning that skipping the reconciliation layer risks presenting a false level of precision on client margin impact and negotiation options.
OE 25: Add a comment to the Heartland reconciliation Linear ticket (issue_heartland_q1_recon) using linear_create_comment, summarizing Hana's latest findings (dispute broader than original 3 items, 3 of 5 Q1 moves have exceptions including unauthorized storage charge), Marcus's three-bucket categorization framework, and the current dispute status including Jake Loomis tying Q3 rate increases to the unresolved Q1 matter.
OE 26: Post a vendor exposure summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006") covering both vendor rate increases (Swift's immediate 15% plus $500 DOT surcharge, Heartland's 20% effective July 1 tied to Q1 dispute), Lakeline's backup capacity limited to Midwest/Southeast corridors with no West Coast or international coverage, and practical implications for coordinators on upcoming move scheduling and vendor selection.
OE 27: Send an email to Marcus Thorne using send_email (sender: "chloe.vance@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"]) with consolidated operational data: Heartland Q1 dispute status ($4,800 overbilling for Marchetti/Osei/Venkatesh, payment hold in place, Hana's findings that the problem extends beyond the original dispute), Swift rate increase (15% base plus $500 DOT surcharge, effective immediately), Heartland rate increase (20% across all tiers, effective July 1, tied to dispute), Lakeline backup capacity (Midwest/Southeast corridors only, ~10% below Heartland pricing, cannot cover West Coast or international), and Alejandro's financial exposure status (Q3 vendor-cost exposure model started but flagging that a defensible client-by-client margin forecast is not yet possible because Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM data do not reconcile cleanly, output is currently directional ranges and assumptions, not final numbers).

16 The email body to Marcus includes Alejandro Fuentes's cost exposure work: that he started a Q3 vendor-cost exposure model but cannot yet produce a defensible client-by-client margin forecast because the data lives in three systems (Airtable, QuickBooks, CRM) that do not reconcile cleanly (or similar).
